channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Profile of Chinese Actor Zhang Linghe, Lead in 'Pursuit of Jade'
item.description: Chinese actor Zhang Linghe has been gaining attention as the lead star of the popular CDrama 'Pursuit of Jade.' Known for his versatility and on-screen charisma, Zhang has captivated fans both in Chin...

item.title: John Ekanem showcases faith and friendship in new film 'Brothers in Arms'
item.description: Nigerian actor and producer John Tyler Ekanem don bring out him new film, Brothers in Arms, wey dey show for him channel, John Ekanem TV from April 5, 2026. The film na faith-based story wey get corre...

item.title: Tanzania pushes clean cooking initiatives to protect health and environment
item.description: Tanzania is working hard to make sure that by 2034, at least 80 percent of its people use clean cooking energy. This move is meant to cut down on pollution, protect the environment, and improve people...

item.title: Ministry Clarifies No Involvement in RCC’s Proposed N$500 Million Loan for Stadiums
item.description: The Ministry of Education, Innovation, Youth, Sport, Arts and Culture in Namibia has clarified that it is not aware of the Roads Contractor Company (RCC) requesting a N$500 million loan from the Devel...

item.title: Haryana revises wages for contractual workers after protests in Gurugram
item.description: The Haryana government has announced a revision of wages for contractual employees who have been striking in Gurugram, demanding fair pay and improved working conditions. The protest, which saw partic...

item.title: US and Iran Agree to Temporary Ceasefire Amid Ongoing Regional Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have agreed to a fragile two-week ceasefire, offering a temporary reprieve to global markets and easing concerns over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for oil, gas, an...

item.title: Marilyn Gladu switches from Conservative to Liberal, sparking questions about her political integrity
item.description: Ontario MP Marilyn Gladu has stunned many by crossing the floor from the Conservative Party to the Liberals, despite having been a vocal critic of the Liberal government in the past. Gladu, who has co...

item.title: Traders Affected by Simu 2000 Market Fire in Dar es Salaam Call for Fast Verification and Financial Help
item.description: Traders whose stalls were destroyed by the recent fire at Simu 2000 Market in Dar es Salaam are asking the government to speed up the process of verifying genuine business owners and providing financi...

item.title: Bien and Alikiba’s collaboration ‘Finale’ sets record as East African music hit
item.description: Kenyan singer and songwriter Bien-Aimé Baraza has praised Tanzanian music star Alikiba for their fruitful collaboration on the track “Finale”, which has quickly become Bien’s most successful solo rele...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch criticises Keir Starmer's lack of a defence plan while visiting the Gulf
item.description: Kemi Badenoch has accused Sir Keir Starmer of prioritising diplomatic gestures over concrete action to strengthen Britain's military. As Starmer visits Saudi Arabia, Badenoch highlighted the governmen...

item.title: Long Island architect admits to eight murders in Gilgo Beach case
item.description: Rex Heuermann, a 62-year-old architect from Long Island, has pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and confessed to an eighth in the infamous Gilgo Beach killings, which spanned over 17 years. Appea...

item.title: 'Ketamine Supplier Receives 15-Year Prison Term for Role in Matthew Perry's Overdose'
item.description: Jayvee Sangha, a 42-year-old dual U.S.-British citizen nicknamed the 'Ketamine Queen,' was sentenced to 15 years in federal prison for her involvement in the 2023 death of actor Matthew Perry. Sangha ...

item.title: Dean Penney on trial for first-degree murder after mother raises questions about another potential suspect
item.description: Dean Penney is currently on trial at the Supreme Court of Newfoundland and Labrador in Corner Brook for the first-degree murder of his estranged wife, Jennifer Hillier-Penney. The case has drawn atten...

item.title: Danny Williams Donated Over $46K to N.L. Tories Amid Lack of Donation Limits
item.description: Former Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Danny Williams made significant donations to the Progressive Conservative party ahead of the 2025 election, contributing over $46,000. In a province where ther...

item.title: Sergeant Fannie Nkosi denied bail following discovery of explosives, firearms, cash and case documents
item.description: Sergeant Fannie Nkosi was refused bail by the Pretoria High Court after a police raid uncovered explosives, seven licensed firearms, large quantities of ammunition, six case dockets, and R50 000 in ca...

item.title: VP Duterte Calls Foreign Interference in PH Justice a Direct Challenge to Filipino Freedom
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte, speaking during the commemoration of the Day of Valor, strongly denounced any foreign attempts to interfere with the justice system of the Philippines. She emphasized that...

item.title: Call for Increase in Motorcycle Taxi Cap Amid Transport Shortage
item.description: Former LTFRB Chairman Winston Ginez is urging the Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) to increase the cap on motorcycle taxis in Metro Manila. Currently, only 45,000 motorcycl...

item.title: Centre argues courts lack authority to classify religious practices as superstition in Sabarimala case
item.description: The Union government has told the Supreme Court that secular courts have no jurisdiction to decide whether a religious practice amounts to superstition. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, representing th...

item.title: NDDC Champions Public-Private Partnerships to Boost Aviation and Economic Growth in Niger Delta
item.description: The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) has renewed its call for stronger public-private partnerships (PPP) as the key to unlocking the economic potential of the Niger Delta. In a strategic meet...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney and astronaut Jeremy Hansen to discuss Canada’s role in Artemis II lunar mission
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to speak with Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen following Artemis II, the first crewed lunar mission to include a Canadian astronaut. The conversation will highlight C...
